score.blocked_reason: Attachment stops mid-Agent-10 and omits the rest of Agents 10-12.

results.evidence: Trading workspace preserves narrow left watchlist, dominant central chart, and secondary right operations panel.
Global and component styles use regular labels, bold values, and extra-bold brand/signal headings.
Signal Intelligence BUY/SELL/HOLD badge now uses an 18px signal value.
Color use remains semantic for buy, sell, warning, info, brand, base surfaces, and borders.
Icon-only controls received aria labels; decorative SVG/lucide icons are hidden where text already labels the control.
Sections use spacing, surface depth, and labels in addition to borders.
Type scale normalization increases separation between labels, values, tabs, and signal badges.
Only Inter and JetBrains Mono are used by authored dashboard styles.
Disallowed sub-10px and fractional arbitrary font sizes were normalized to the allowed scale.
Dense table rows retain compact line-height while signal and empty-state text uses readable leading.
Tracking remains on uppercase labels and compact metadata only.
No authored dashboard source contains text below 10px.
font-mono-data and numeric-data now enforce JetBrains Mono and tabular numerals.
Bold weight remains reserved for headings, prices, statuses, and signal/action values.
Text is not placed directly over image or decorative gradient backgrounds.
Large areas use base surface colors; signal colors remain limited to badges, values, and state accents.
Buy, sell, warn, info, and brand colors keep one semantic role each.
Primary text remains high-contrast on dark dashboard surfaces.
Secondary chrome text remains restricted to muted metadata and labels.
Color-coded states also include visible text, status labels, aria labels, or position.
No decorative extra hue family was added; the semantic palette is unchanged.
Sub-4px spacing utilities in audited dashboard source were normalized to 4px-grid equivalents.
Panel padding remains compact while external section gaps are larger.
Headers and logical groups keep clear spacing between unrelated controls.
Rows remain data-dense; critical action buttons were raised to safer minimum hit areas.
Empty states, logs, risk metrics, and action controls remain inside visible parent groups.
Global focus-visible and reusable interactive-control states were added.
Global focus-visible ring applies to keyboard-reachable controls.
Slow duration utilities were normalized to 100ms.
interactive-control and btn-active-scale provide tactile active feedback.
Disabled action buttons use visible opacity and not-allowed cursor.
Command palette items are semantic buttons; display-only topbar market context no longer uses pointer styling.
High-risk Trading action buttons use min-height 44px.
Interactive-control centralizes 100ms color/background/border transitions.
UI feedback uses 100ms transitions; tick flash remains 400ms; skeleton loading is a functional loading loop.
Data and loading animations use ease-out or appropriate timing.
Infinite animation is limited to live/loading/spinner states.
prefers-reduced-motion block disables long-running animations and transitions.
Workspace order remains Trading, Research, Strategy, Risk, Ops.
Active workspace tab, active watchlist item, symbol context, and OHLCV chart overlay remain visible.
Signal conditions, signal stats, risk metrics, and account info remain spatially grouped.
Detailed ops and log information stays below/secondary to the primary chart.
Log empty states now include what is empty, why, and the next action.
Signal Intelligence remains visible in the Trading right panel.
Workspace tabs remain 5 and log tabs were reduced to 5.
Default symbol BTCUSDT and default workspace Trading are preserved.
Stale status now includes prescriptive tooltip text.
Settings form controls use visible labels linked to input IDs.
Manual buy/sell and safety block controls require hold-to-confirm with progress text.
Topbar keeps API, DB, Redis, stale, and symbol status visible.
Canvas chart now supports a skeleton loading state.
CSS active states, hold progress, tab changes, and data updates provide immediate feedback.
Stale state mutes price context and changes API health to warning/offline semantics.
Toast hierarchy supports info, warning, error, and success with distinct semantic styling and aria-live.
Command palette options are buttons, settings modal traps focus, and interactive controls are keyboard reachable.
The provided attachment ends mid-Agent-10 and does not include complete acceptance criteria for the rest of Agent-10, Agent-11, or Agent-12.
